headline: Samsung and SK Hynix tumble 7%+ as chip rout spreads
detail: This is directly hitting your book. A 7%+ drop in the world's largest memory chipmakers signals real sector stress, not noise - it tends to drag US semis (SMH, SOXL) and chip-adjacent names with it. For you on margin, SMH and especially SOXL are the immediate bleed points.
rec: Consider trimming SOXL toward your $200 stop rather than waiting for it to get hit passively - in a 3X leveraged name during an active chip rout, passive stop management can cost extra slippage. SMH hold but watch $540 stop.

headline: Amazon designing its own AI chips for Echo and Fire TV
detail: This is a mild negative signal for incumbent chip suppliers - in-house silicon means less revenue for merchant chip makers over time. It reinforces the broader trend of big tech reducing dependency on third-party chip names, which is a slow headwind for your SMH/SOXL holdings.
rec: No immediate trade warranted - this is a slow-burn structural story, not a today catalyst. Keep watching whether CRWD or other software names decouple positively from the chip selloff as money rotates.

headline: Russia massive strike on Ukraine, Poland scrambles jets
detail: Geopolitical escalation spikes risk-off sentiment broadly, pressuring growth and tech names. Your book is 90% concentrated in exactly the names that get sold first when macro fear spikes. Margin exposure means drawdowns hit harder than the index moves suggest.
rec: No single trade, but this adds pressure on top of the chip rout - today is a day to shrink risk, not add it. If you were thinking of adding anything from the scanner, wait for this to settle.

headline: BYD and Xiaomi surge on June delivery figures
detail: Strong China EV/tech delivery numbers show consumer tech demand in Asia is holding up, which is a mild positive read-through for semiconductor demand longer term. However, this is not a direct catalyst for your US-listed holdings today given the chip rout dominating the tape.
rec: Nothing actionable for your book right now - the positive China tech read is overwhelmed by the Samsung/SK Hynix selloff dominating the sector narrative today.
